new_classes.definition_en: A spirit distilled from fermented sugar-cane molasses or sweet plant juices and rice, using a special yeast. Arrack is distinct from raki (which is obtained by redistilling raisin or dry fig spirits with aniseed and falls under subheading 2208 90 56 or 2208 90 69).

new_classes.label_de: Arrak in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 L (Unterposition 2208 90 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Arrack (a spirit produced from sugar-cane molasses or sweet plant juices and rice using a special yeast) presented in containers with a nominal liquid volume of not more than 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2208 90 11.

new_classes.label_en: Arrack distillation process (fermentation of sugar-cane molasses or sweet plant juices and rice with special yeast, followed by distillation)
new_classes.label_de: Arrak-Destillationsprozess (Vergärung von Zuckerrohrmelasse oder zuckerhaltigen Pflanzensäften und Reis mit einer besonderen Hefe, gefolgt von Destillation)
new_classes.definition_en: Production process for arrack: fermentation of sugar-cane molasses or the sweet juices of plants together with rice, using a special yeast, followed by distillation to yield an alcoholic spirit.

Quality borne by an arrack spirit indicating that its raw material is sugar-cane molasses or the sweet juices of plants (together with rice), fermented with a special yeast, as required for classification under CN 2208 90 11/19.

coverage_explanation:
The axioms capture the key distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 90 11 (Arrack in containers ≤ 2 L):
1. ✅ Arrack as a class of spirit under heading 2208, distinct from rum (sugar-cane-based but with different process) and raki.
2. ✅ Production process: fermentation of sugar-cane molasses or sweet plant juices + rice with a special yeast, followed by distillation (ArrackDistillationProcess).
3. ✅ Raw-material quality: sugar-cane molasses or sweet plant juice origin (SugarCaneMolassesOrSweetPlantJuiceOriginQuality).
4. ✅ Container size ≤ 2 L expressed via hasPartsome SmallContainer on ArrackSmallContainer22089011.
5. ⚠️ The complement restriction on SugarCaneOriginQuality is added to ensure Arrack is not confused with rum, but this is a soft discrimination and may be too blunt — reduced score accordingly.
6. ⚠️ The rice-ingredient component of arrack production is not modelled as a separate ingredient class (no RiceIngredient class proposed), resulting in partial coverage of the raw-material criterion.
7. ⚠️ The 'special yeast' requirement is captured only implicitly via the ArrackDistillationProcess definition, not as a separate quality axiom.
8. ⚠️ The explicit distinction from raki (subheading 2208 90 56/69) is noted in the definition but not axiomatised as a disjointness restriction, as the Raki class does not yet exist in the TBox.
